backlog.title: Restore Lane Isolation — Remove META Passthrough
backlog.description: Eliminate the META command class from the agent-browser-pool dispatch pipeline. The META passthrough (pool_dispatch_classify + pool_wrapper_main step-c) was supposed to be removed in the Phase-2 delta but was never implemented, leaving a critical isolation breach: meta commands (skills, mcp, dashboard, plugin, session list, --version, flags-only) bypass session-forcing and owner-resolution, enabling cross-lane access via --session. This phase removes the META class from code, updates all tests to assert the new driving-only contract, and syncs all documentation to the pool-verbs-vs-driving model.

milestones.title: Core Engine — Remove META Classification & Passthrough
milestones.description: Delete the step-c META passthrough block from pool_wrapper_main (the security fix), then delete the now-dead pool_dispatch_classify function and its contract comment. After this milestone, every non-pool-verb token flows through the driving path: owner resolve → fail-fast without pi → acquire/reuse lane → normalize args → strip --session → force AGENT_BROWSER_SESSION → exec.

tasks.title: Remove META passthrough execution path and dispatch classifier
tasks.description: Two-part fix: (1) Delete step-c (the classify call + meta exec block) from pool_wrapper_main so all non-pool-verb tokens enter the driving path. (2) Delete pool_dispatch_classify entirely since it is now dead code (always would return 'driving'), and remove its selftest from validate.sh.

subtasks.title: Delete step-c META passthrough block from pool_wrapper_main
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/002_97982899bef6/bugfix/001_2f350a0ce445/architecture/system_context.md § 'Code Change Map' and research_meta_refs.md § 2. The step-c META block is at lib/pool.sh:3529–3536. It reads: `class="$(pool_dispatch_classify "$@")"` (line 3532) then `if [[ "$class" == "meta" ]]; then _pool_log ...; exec "$POOL_REAL_BIN" "$@"; fi` (lines 3533–3536). This block executes meta commands UNCHANGED (original argv, no session strip, no session force) BEFORE owner resolution (step d). The `class` local variable is declared at lib/pool.sh:3518: `local class N port _has_json _a`. The bin/agent-browser-pool dispatcher (lines 30–37) already catches status|reap|release|doctor|--help|-h|help as pool verbs BEFORE pool_wrapper_main — so only non-pool-verb tokens reach here.
2. INPUT: pool_wrapper_main receives "$@" (the agent's original argv). After this subtask, step a (config+state+preflight, lines 3519–3527) runs, then control flows DIRECTLY to step d (owner resolution, line 3538) with NO intervening dispatch/classify step.
3. LOGIC: (a) Delete lines 3529–3536 (the entire step-c block: comment header `# --- c. dispatch...`, the `class="$(...)"` assignment, the `if [[ "$class" == "meta" ]]` block with its log + exec). (b) Remove `class` from the local declaration at line 3518 — change `local class N port _has_json _a` to `local N port _has_json _a`. (c) Update the pool_wrapper_main header comment block (lib/pool.sh:3462–3515): remove the line at ~3468 that says `# - M6.T1.S1 pool_dispatch_classify   (step c: meta vs driving)` and remove/update the GOTCHA at ~3499–3501 about passthrough exec passing original argv. (d) Update _pool_preflight_real_bin comment (lib/pool.sh:3439–3440) that says 'meta commands (skills/--version/…) exec it too' — change to note only driving commands exec it now. (e) Verify with `bash -n lib/pool.sh` (syntax clean) and `shellcheck -s bash lib/pool.sh` (0 findings). DO NOT run real Chrome or the test suite.
4. OUTPUT: pool_wrapper_main no longer has a step-c; the local `class` variable is gone; all non-pool-verb tokens flow through step d (owner resolve → fail-fast). The function pool_dispatch_classify still EXISTS (deleted in S2) but is now dead code (no call site). This is acceptable — S2 removes it.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Rewrite .agents/skills/agent-browser-pool/references/configuration.md § 'Command dispatch: meta vs. driving' (lines 44–76). Replace the 3-step decision (meta→passthrough, no-pi→fail-fast, driving→lane) with a 2-step model: pool verbs (status/reap/release/doctor/help, caught by bin/agent-browser-pool) → admin functions; everything else → DRIVING (owner resolve → fail-fast without pi → acquire lane → strip --session → force AGENT_BROWSER_SESSION → exec). Remove the 'Meta commands (passthrough — never acquire a lane)' subsection entirely. Update line 8 (lists pool_dispatch_classify among implementing functions) to remove pool_dispatch_classify. This rides WITH the work — do not create a separate docs subtask.

subtasks.title: Delete pool_dispatch_classify function, contract comment, and obsolete selftest
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See research_meta_refs.md § 1 and § 4. pool_dispatch_classify is defined at lib/pool.sh:3070–3128 (function body) with its contract comment block at lib/pool.sh:3012–3069 (the `# ===...# Wrapper shim — command dispatch (P1.M6.T1.S1)` block). After S1 deleted the step-c call site, this function is dead code. The function is PURE (reads no globals, writes no files, returns 0 always). Other functions reference it in comments only: pool_normalize_close header (lib/pool.sh:3176 'mirrors pool_dispatch_classify'), pool_strip_session_args header (lib/pool.sh:3352 'mirrors pool_dispatch_classify'), _pool_clean_args_is_bare_connect header (lib/pool.sh:3668 'mirrors pool_normalize_connect / pool_dispatch_classify'), _pool_clean_args_is_close header (lib/pool.sh:3717 'mirroring pool_dispatch_classify'). The selftest selftest_dispatch_classify_cases is at test/validate.sh:345–384 — it calls pool_dispatch_classify directly; once the function is deleted, this selftest will error. validate.sh uses compgen-based discovery: `for fn in $(compgen -A function | grep '^selftest_')` — simply deleting the function removes it from discovery.
2. INPUT: S1 has removed the only call site (step-c). pool_dispatch_classify is dead code. The selftest_dispatch_classify_cases function in test/validate.sh is the only remaining consumer.
3. LOGIC: (a) Delete the pool_dispatch_classify contract comment block (lib/pool.sh:3012–3069, the `# ===...# Wrapper shim — command dispatch` section header through the GOTCHA notes ending before `pool_dispatch_classify() {` at line 3070). (b) Delete the pool_dispatch_classify function body (lib/pool.sh:3070–3128, from `pool_dispatch_classify() {` through its closing `}`). (c) Update comment references in OTHER functions that say 'mirrors pool_dispatch_classify': at lib/pool.sh:3176 reword to 'mirrors the flag-scan pattern' or similar; at lib/pool.sh:3352, 3668, 3717 likewise. Do NOT change the scanning LOGIC of those functions — only update the comments that reference the deleted function name. (d) Delete selftest_dispatch_classify_cases (test/validate.sh:345–384) entirely — the function it tests no longer exists. Remove the function definition and its header comment block. (e) Verify: `bash -n lib/pool.sh` and `bash -n test/validate.sh` (syntax clean); `shellcheck -s bash lib/pool.sh` and `shellcheck -s bash test/validate.sh` (0 findings); `grep -n 'pool_dispatch_classify' lib/pool.sh test/validate.sh` should return ZERO hits. DO NOT run real Chrome or the test suite.
4. OUTPUT: lib/pool.sh no longer defines or references pool_dispatch_classify. test/validate.sh no longer has selftest_dispatch_classify_cases. The bin/agent-browser-pool case statement remains the single source of truth for pool-verb/driving classification.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update .agents/skills/agent-browser-pool/SKILL.md § 'Which commands trigger a lane' (lines 55–65): remove the 'meta commands pass straight through' clause (lines 61–63) and replace with 'Every command except pool verbs (status/reap/release/doctor/help) is a driving command — it resolves your pi owner, acquires/reuses your lane, and runs scoped to abpool-<N> with --session stripped.' Update the reference pointer at lines 143–145 to say 'pool-verbs-vs-driving dispatch' instead of 'meta-vs-driving dispatch'. This rides WITH the work.

milestones.title: Test Contract Update — Assert Driving-Only Behavior
milestones.description: Replace the two transparency.sh tests that assert the old META-passthrough contract (byte-equal to real binary) with tests that assert the new contract: non-pool-verb tokens (skills, --version, mcp, session list) fail-fast without a pi ancestor. Update the file header checklist to match.

tasks.title: Replace META-passthrough assertions in transparency.sh with fail-fast assertions
tasks.description: test_passthrough_skills (line 236) and test_version_passthrough (line 270) currently assert byte-equality with the real binary (META passthrough). Replace them with assertions that skills/--version fail-fast with the 'pi ancestor' message when invoked without a pi ancestor, mirroring the existing test_driving_no_pi_ancestor_fails_fast pattern.

subtasks.title: Replace test_passthrough_skills and test_version_passthrough with fail-fast tests + update header
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See research_test_framework.md and research_meta_refs.md § 4. transparency.sh uses a single-setup runner (_abpool_run_transparency_suite, lines 533–573) with ONE setup() call; bodies run via `if "$fn"; then` in the MAIN shell (NOT subshell — no mid-suite EXIT trap). Test discovery: `compgen -A function | grep '^test_'`. The existing fail-fast pattern is test_driving_no_pi_ancestor_fails_fast (lines ~480–510): it calls _transparency_setup_real_env (sets AGENT_BROWSER_REAL so preflight passes), does NOT call _transparency_spawn_owner (no pi ancestor), uses `env -u AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_OWNER_PID -u AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_OWNER_STARTTIME setsid --fork "$ABPOOL_ADMIN" <cmd>`, captures output to a temp file, polls for 'pi ancestor' in the message. This pattern is REQUIRED because previous test bodies export AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_OWNER_PID in the main shell; `env -u` strips it and setsid --fork reparents the child away from the pi/bash chain. Current tests to replace: test_passthrough_skills (lines 236–243, asserts byte-equal skills output) and test_version_passthrough (lines 270–277, asserts byte-equal --version output). Header checklist at lines 8–12 documents these as '(a) skills get core → passthrough' and '(b2) --version → passthrough'. Inline comments at lines 229–234 (TEST (a) header) and lines 265–268 (TEST (b2) header) describe the meta-passthrough rationale.
2. INPUT: P1.M1.T1.S1 has removed step-c from pool_wrapper_main. After the code fix, `agent-browser-pool skills get core` and `agent-browser-pool --version` (with no pi ancestor) hit step d (owner resolve → POOL_OWNER_PID==0 → pool_die with 'pi ancestor' message). The fail-fast happens BEFORE any Chrome/lane work → sub-second, no hang.
3. LOGIC: (a) Rename test_passthrough_skills → test_skills_fail_fast_no_pi (or similar). Replace its body with the fail-fast pattern: call _transparency_setup_real_env, do NOT spawn owner, run `env -u AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_OWNER_PID -u AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_OWNER_STARTTIME setsid --fork "$ABPOOL_ADMIN" skills get core`, capture output, assert it contains 'pi ancestor'. Update the inline comment (lines 229–234) to describe the new contract: skills is a driving command, not meta; it fail-fasts without a pi ancestor. (b) Rename test_version_passthrough → test_version_fail_fast_no_pi (or similar). Same fail-fast pattern but with `--version` as the command. Update inline comment (lines 265–268). (c) Update header checklist (lines 8–12): change '(a) skills get core → passthrough (META → exec real binary; byte-equal)' to '(a) skills get core → FAIL-FAST (driving, no pi ancestor; §2.4 step 1)' and '(b2) --version → passthrough (META → exec real binary; byte-equal)' to '(b2) --version → FAIL-FAST (driving, no pi ancestor; §2.4 step 1)'. (d) Verify: `bash -n test/transparency.sh` and `shellcheck -s bash test/transparency.sh` (syntax/lint clean). `grep -nE 'passthrough|meta' test/transparency.sh` should return ZERO hits (or only hits in comments about what was REMOVED, if any contextual note is added). DO NOT run the test suite against the shared sandbox (AGENTS.md §1).
4. OUTPUT: transparency.sh asserts that skills and --version fail-fast without a pi ancestor, consistent with all other driving commands. The suite no longer passes on the old (buggy) code.
5. DOCS: none — test files are internal; no user-facing/config/API surface change. The transparency.sh header IS the test documentation and is updated inline per (c) above.

milestones.title: Sync Changeset-Level Documentation (Mode B)
milestones.description: Final cross-cutting documentation sweep. The README.md (root user docs) describes the dispatch model in 4 sections (Usage, Classification detail, How it works diagram, Troubleshooting). All reference the removed META class and must be updated to the pool-verbs-vs-driving model. This is a Mode B task because these are overview/whole-feature docs that only make sense once the entire changeset is in place.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation
tasks.description: Update all README.md sections that reference META commands, passthrough, or the meta-vs-driving dispatch model. Also perform a final consistency sweep across all files to confirm zero stale references to the removed META class.

subtasks.title: Update README.md META references and perform final consistency sweep
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See research_skill_docs.md § 'README.md (root user docs)'. README.md references META in 4 locations: (1) Line 95: 'Pool verbs (...) and META commands work from any shell.' (2) Lines 135–141: Classification detail blockquote listing META tokens (--version, skills, dashboard, plugin, mcp, session list, flags-only) that 'pass through to the real agent-browser unchanged, acquiring no lane'. (3) Lines 253–294: 'How it works' section with a classify diagram at lines 258–270 that has a META branch (lines 263–266: 'META (...)? → passthrough to the real binary (no lane)') and step 2 at line 277 ('classify — pool verb? META command? → handled above (no lane)'). (4) Lines 313–317: Troubleshooting — 'Pool verbs (...) and META commands work from any shell.'
2. INPUT: P1.M1.T1.S1 has removed the META passthrough from code; P1.M1.T1.S2 has deleted pool_dispatch_classify and updated configuration.md + SKILL.md; P1.M2.T1.S1 has updated transparency.sh tests. The new contract: pool verbs (status/reap/release/doctor/help) work from any shell; ALL other tokens (including --version, skills, mcp, dashboard, plugin, session list, flags-only) are DRIVING commands that require a pi ancestor and fail-fast without one.
3. LOGIC: (a) Line 95: Remove the META clause — change to 'Pool verbs (status / doctor / reap / release / help) work from any shell. All other commands require a pi ancestor (they are driving commands routed to your lane).' (b) Lines 135–141: Replace the Classification detail blockquote. Remove the META token list. State that pool verbs are caught by bin/agent-browser-pool's case statement; everything else (including --version, skills, mcp, etc.) is a driving command that resolves the owner, acquires a lane, strips --session, and forces AGENT_BROWSER_SESSION. (c) Lines 253–294: Update the 'How it works' classify diagram — remove the META branch entirely. The flow becomes: bin/agent-browser-pool catches pool verbs → pool_admin_*; everything else → pool_wrapper_main → owner resolve (fail-fast without pi) → acquire/reuse lane → normalize → strip --session → force session → exec. Update step 2 at line 277 to remove 'META command?' from the classify description. (d) Lines 313–317: Remove the META reference in the troubleshooting section. (e) Final consistency sweep: run `grep -rnE 'meta|passthrough|META|dispatch_classify' lib/pool.sh bin/agent-browser-pool test/*.sh .agents/skills/agent-browser-pool/**/*.md README.md` — the ONLY acceptable remaining hits are: (i) 'meta' as a common English word in unrelated context, (ii) owner-passthrough concept at lib/pool.sh:580–581,1005,2089–2099 (UNRELATED — do NOT touch these). If any stale META-dispatch references remain, fix them.
4. OUTPUT: README.md describes the pool-verbs-vs-driving model with zero references to META passthrough. All files in the repo are consistent: no stale references to the removed META command class.
5. DOCS: This IS the documentation task (Mode B). No further doc subtask is needed — this is the final sweep.
